channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: A Definition of Good Explanations and the Challenges Explaining LLM Outputs
item.description: arXiv:2606.14838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: How to define a good explanation is a long-standing philosophical debate which has found recent renewed interest in the context of AI outputs. Explainability is crucial for AI adoption in many contexts, but in order to produce good explanations of AI...

item.title: Dr-DCI: Scaling Direct Corpus Interaction via Dynamic Workspace Expansion
item.description: arXiv:2606.14885v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic search over large corpora relies on retriever-mediated interfaces (e.g., BM25 or ColBERT) for scalable candidate discovery. While effective at ranking relevant documents, these interfaces expose evidence only as ranked results or bounded...

item.title: Relational Structural Causal Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.14892v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: An artificial intelligence must have a model of its environment that is causal, supporting reasoning about interventions and counterfactuals, and also combinatorial, supporting generalization to unseen combinations of objects. In this work, we...

item.title: Trust Between AI Agents: Measuring Formation, Breakage, and Recovery, with Implications for Governing Multi-Agent Systems
item.description: arXiv:2606.14923v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As language-model agents increasingly work in teams, each agent must decide how much to trust its teammates. Yet we lack a standard way to measure trust between AI agents. We propose a behavioral measure based on costly verification. In a cooperative...

item.title: PrologMCP: A Standardized Prolog Tool Interface for LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2606.14935v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Frontier reasoning-tuned language models still fail on deductive tasks at depth, and the cost of improved performance through extended internal reasoning scales poorly. Symbolic delegation offers a complementary route: a language model translates the...

item.title: QPILOTS: Efficient Test-Time Q-Steering for Flow Policies
item.description: arXiv:2606.14801v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Flow-matching and diffusion policies are expressive action generators, but optimizing them with temporal-difference reinforcement learning (RL) remains difficult. Effective policy extraction requires exploiting the critic's action gradient, yet...

item.title: GRAPE: Guided Parameter-Space Evolution for Compact Adversarial Robustness
item.description: arXiv:2606.14865v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Adversarial Training (AT) improves neural network robustness, but most methods train a fixed parameter space from the start. This paper asks whether the order in which parameters become optimizable can affect the final robust solution, even when the...

item.title: {\alpha}-Fair Insurance Pricing: A Fairness Continuum
item.description: arXiv:2606.14898v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fairness in insurance pricing remains a long-standing and deeply debated puzzle. On one hand, insurers, driven by profitability considerations, set premiums that differentiate across individual risks to achieve actuarial fairness. On the other hand,...

item.title: GRASP: Gradient-Aligned Sequential Parameter Transfer for Memory-Efficient Multi-Source Learning
item.description: arXiv:2606.14900v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-source transfer learning faces a fundamental scalability bottleneck: existing approaches require either loading all K source models into memory simultaneously during parameter fusion, requiring O(K) memory, or deploying all models at inference...

item.title: Policy Regret for Embedding Model Routing: Contextual Bandits with Low-Rank Experts
item.description: arXiv:2606.14929v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern recommendation systems increasingly rely on dynamically routing diverse queries to multiple embedding models. Despite its practical significance, this problem remains poorly understood under realistic conditions like adversarial queries, bandit...

item.title: PhoneHarness: Harnessing Phone-Use Agents through Mixed GUI, CLI, and Tool Actions
item.description: arXiv:2606.14832v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Phone agents are increasingly expected to complete real mobile workflows rather than merely predict the next screen action. However, much of the current mobile-agent literature still evaluates agents primarily as GUI controllers that observe a screen,...

item.title: Evaluating the Robustness of Proof Autoformalization in Lean 4
item.description: arXiv:2606.14867v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Proof autoformalization aims to translate a mathematical informal proof written in natural language into a formal proof in a formal language such as Lean~4. Several works have developed LLM-based models for proof autoformalization. However, existing...

item.title: Context Compression Is Not One Thing: Readable Symbolic Re-expression vs. Coherent Summary at Matched Budget
item.description: arXiv:2606.14875v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study context compression for multi-hop question answering with small language models. We propose Telegraph English, a readable symbolic format that rewrites retrieved passages into structured entity-relation statements, preserving reasoning...

item.title: Simplifying the Modeling of Arbitrary Conditionals in Natural Language
item.description: arXiv:2606.14943v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Causal Transformers model sequences through an autoregressive factorization of the joint distribution, which enables efficient left-to-right decoding and conditional likelihood computation. However, they cannot tractably sample from or evaluate...

item.title: CoRA: Confidence-Rationale Alignment for Reliable Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2606.14961v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning can improve LLM performance, but high answer confidence may be misleading when the accompanying CoT rationale is plausible yet incomplete or poorly supported. We study confidence--rationale alignment: whether a model's...

item.title: [AINews] Satya on Loopcraft: Building Frontier Ecosystems
item.description: a quiet day lets us report on Satya's hit essay

item.title: Alibaba's Internal AI Agent Battle: QoderWork vs Wukong for Enterprise Crown
item.description: Alibaba's QoderWork outperforms DingTalk-based Wukong internally, as the company races Tencent and ByteDance to establish a flagship enterprise AI agent product.

item.title: China's Tech Giants Deploy AI Across Every Front in the 618 Shopping Festival
item.description: Alibaba, JD.com, and ByteDance put AI at the center of China's 618 shopping festival, with AI assistants, digital hosts, and smart logistics reshaping e-commerce.

item.title: China Achieves Breakthrough in Silicon-28 Mass Production for Quantum Computing
item.description: Chinese scientists have achieved a major breakthrough in stable isotope enrichment and high-purity silicon preparation, successfully producing silicon-28 isotop...

item.title: Alibaba's AI Glasses Top China Sales Rankings at Just RMB 1,997
item.description: Alibaba has emerged as the early winner in China's rapidly growing AI glasses market, with its Tongyi Qianwen (Qwen) AI glasses ranking first in nationwide sale...

item.title: Tungsten Hexafluoride Prices Double as AI Computing Demand Reshapes Semiconductor Materials Supply
item.description: AI-driven demand pushes tungsten hexafluoride prices to double in six months, with global supply tightening as export controls and production cuts reshape semiconductor materials landscape.

item.title: Users may experience issue with Codex Cloud tasks
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Codex Web (Operational)

item.title: FedRAMP workspaces and API orgs have degraded performance
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services.Affected components FedRAMP (Degraded performance)

item.title: Chinese Hackers Abused Google Workspace Rules to Steal Research and Defense Emails
item.description: A China-linked espionage group hid inside North American medical, academic, and military research networks for more than a year, quietly stealing sensitive research and defense email. The way in was a backdoor on their REDCap research servers that stole login credentials. The exfiltration was the...

item.title: North Korean Hackers Are Turning Developer Tools Into Malware Delivery Channels
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged two malicious cyber campaigns that exhibit similarities with a persistent North Korean threat cluster known as Contagious Interview (aka Famous Chollima, HexagonalRodent, and Void Dokkaebi). According to a report published by Proofpoint, the threat actor has...

item.title: INC20000034
item.description: Jun 15, 19:22 UTC Monitoring - Current status: Services remain stable following our previously implemented mitigation actions; however, customers could experience performance issues until the underlying issue is resolved. We are closely monitoring the environment while we continue to collaborate...

item.title: Multiple services have elevated errors and endpoint failures when checking feature flags
item.description: Jun 15, 19:10 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jun 15, 19:10 UTC Update - We identified an issue with feature flag checks that caused...

item.title: Why do South Koreans love AI so much?
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. When I landed in Seoul after a grueling 12-hour flight from San Francisco, I walked through an unmanned immigration checkpoint, where a machine scanned my face...

item.title: OAuth Account Creation / Login Error
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe have identified that users are experiencing an error when attempting to create an account or logging in using Google OAuth for the impacted services. We are working on implementing a mitigation.Affected components Login (Degraded performance) Login (Degraded performance)

item.title: Increased latency with webhooks
item.description: Jun 15, 17:37 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jun 15, 16:39 UTC Monitoring - The degradation affecting Webhooks has been mitigated. We...

item.title: LiteLLM Vulnerability Chain Lets Low-Privilege Users Take Over AI Gateway Servers
item.description: A default low-privilege account on a LiteLLM proxy can climb to full admin and run code on the server by chaining three vulnerabilities, researchers at Obsidian Security disclosed LiteLLM is a widely deployed open-source AI gateway that brokers calls to more than 100 model providers behind one...

item.title: Ransomware Attack Shuts Down Mills of Australia’s Second-Largest Sugar Producer
item.description: Mackay Sugar was targeted in a cyberattack carried out by a threat group known as The Gentlemen. The post Ransomware Attack Shuts Down Mills of Australia’s Second-Largest Sugar Producer appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: This man with ALS is “the first power user” of a brain implant that lets him speak
item.description: Casey Harrell has had a set of electrodes embedded in his brain for almost three years. Harrell, who has amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) and is paralyzed, first used his brain-computer interface (BCI) to “speak” sentences with the help of a research team in 2023. Since then, Harrell has clocked...

item.title: One-Click Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Steal Emails, Files, and MFA Codes
item.description: A single click on a trusted Microsoft link could have let an attacker pull emails, calendar details, and indexed files out of Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search. Researchers at Varonis Threat Labs chained three bugs into a one-click exfiltration path they call SearchLeak. Because the link...

item.title: Chinese Hackers Target Medical, Military, and AI Research in North America
item.description: Google’s Threat Intelligence Group has been tracking the cyberespionage group as UNC6508 since early 2025. The post Chinese Hackers Target Medical, Military, and AI Research in North America appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Chrome 0-Day, UniFi Exploits, macOS Stealers, VPN Flaw and More
item.description: Stuff broke again. Not in a movie way. An old tool was left exposed. An abandoned package was abused. A deprecated feature was still running in prod. This week is the same lesson in a new form: phishing kits are easier to rent, AI names are useful bait, old login paths still fail, and forgotten...

item.title: NewCore Emerges From Stealth Mode With $66 Million in Funding
item.description: The startup has built a security-first identity platform to protect humans, machines, and AI agents. The post NewCore Emerges From Stealth Mode With $66 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: cutting AC emissions, and nature’s drug designer
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. These new solid-state ACs promise a cool future. Scientists aren’t so sure. After three years of record-­breaking heat and another scorcher underway,...

item.title: Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges
item.description: Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko admitted to working on the development of a loader for the Conti gang. The post Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems
item.description: The pharmaceutical giant says the attackers gained access to personal data stored on the compromised systems. The post Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: These new solid-state ACs promise a cool future. Scientists aren’t so sure.
item.description: After three years of record-­breaking heat, this one is set to be yet another scorcher. Air-conditioning? Not going anywhere. The International Energy Agency projects that the number of AC units will triple by 2050. That’s good for health—one Lancet study estimated that AC prevented nearly 200,000...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Jun 15, 08:56 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 15, 08:14 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 15, 06:20 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Welcome to the AGI era of AI governance
item.description: It's a one-way door and we weren't ready for it.

item.title: Intermittent Slow Cache Responses
item.description: Jun 14, 03:40 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 14, 03:30 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 13, 23:43 UTC Identified - The cause of this issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 13, 23:22 UTC Investigating - We...

item.title: [AINews] Fable and Mythos officially too dangerous to release
item.description: We are in the strangest timeline.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Jun 13, 02:11 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 13, 02:03 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: We’ve suspended access to Claude Mythos 5 and Claude Fable 5
item.description: Jun 13, 00:50 UTC Monitoring - Learn more here: https://anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access

item.title: INC20000033
item.description: Jun 12, 20:57 UTC Resolved - Current status: We've implemented the fix for this issue and monitored the environment to confirm that service was restored. If you experience additional issues or have questions, please open a support case via Snowflake Community or the Support page in...

item.title: olmo-eval: An evaluation workbench for the model development loop
Cloudflare Dashboard and Cloudflare API service issues
item.description: Jun 12, 15:03 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 12, 14:56 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 12, 14:27 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with Cloudflare Dashboard and related APIs....
